commonIssues:
Since November 2022, fingerprinting is mandatory. Many couples experience delays because their fingerprints taken at the time of application do not match those taken on the day of registration, causing the system to lock the application.

How to fix:

  • Ensure your hands are clean and dry during fingerprinting. If mismatched, request a manual override by the SDO or re-capture.

The state's marriage registration portal ("MARREG") has experienced shutdowns and slow performance, resulting in difficulties uploading documents and booking appointments, particularly during data migrations.

How to fix:

  • Try logging into the MARREG Portal during early morning hours and keep documents ready in PDF format.

Mismatches in spelling, name order, or address across documents (Aadhaar, Voter ID, PAN) often lead to applications being rejected or returned by the Sub-Divisional Officer (SDO).

How to fix:

  • Synchronize details across Aadhaar and PAN before applying to avoid SDO rejection.

Couples often apply to the wrong local office, failing to adhere to the rule that the application must be filed within the jurisdiction where at least one party has resided for at least 30 days before registration.

How to fix:

  • Apply exactly in the jurisdiction where one partner has lived for the last 30 days. Provide valid address proof.

Last-minute issues with witnesses not having valid ID proofs or not being present for mandatory physical verification.

How to fix:

  • Ensure witnesses bring original EPIC (Voter ID) or Aadhaar cards for physical verification.

Typing errors in names, dates, or address in the final certificate, which are difficult to correct after the fact.

How to fix:

  • Double-check the draft carefully before final submission.

The law allows errors to be corrected within 30 days of registration. If missed, it requires further legal steps or, in some extreme cases, having to register again.

How to fix:

  • Report and apply for corrections within the 30-day window to avoid complicated legal processes.

Couples who use unauthorized agents often find their marriage certificate is not registered in the official government system, rendering it invalid for legal purposes (e.g., visa applications).

How to fix:

  • Only use the official MARREG portal. Verify the generated certificate number online.

Because records are being migrated to a digital system, some older applications (pre-2023) might face delays or disappear from the system.

How to fix:

  • For pre-2023 applications, visit the local registry office with your old acknowledgment slips to request manual tracking.
